brand.alt: Photo of a bunch of grapes on the plot
brand.intro: "Le Calvaire" is the name the winegrowers of Russin gave to this small plot with extreme slopes, where every intervention was once a true ordeal. Among the most steep vineyards in the canton of Geneva, it is the first that the Domaine took over and replanted; it is at the origin of its history and its exigence.
brand.outro:
Today, Le Calvaire gives its name to our range, in tribute to this founding land and to the man who entrusted it to us.



Our range consists of a white "Sélection" and a red "Grande Réserve", a single-plot cuvée made exclusively from the grapes of the Calvaire plot. They are crafted with the same care on slopes of equal intensity, then produced in limited, numbered quantities.
brand.title: Calvaire. From the slopes, character is born.

text.intro: Geneva has produced bankers, diplomats, watchmakers of genius. It was time it produced a great wine. That is the Domaine Simone project: a wine built for excellence, rooted in an exceptional terroir cultivated with a single ambition: to rival the greatest references in the world.
text.outro: A terroir built for excellence, in the image of the estate's wines.

slope.text:
Slopes of 30 to 40% discipline the vine, encourage deep rooting and induce a controlled water stress, a vector of concentration and complexity.



Facing due West, these plots generously embrace the late afternoon sun, conducive to a slow and complete phenolic ripeness. Half of our vines are old vines, whose deep roots draw patiently from the depths of the terroir, offering a substance of rare intensity and natural complexity that only time can build.

soil.text: Registered in the AOC Premier Cru Côtes de Russin, our plots rest on generously stony clay-silt soils, a legacy of the great glacial migrations of the Quaternary and the Tertiary molasse. A balanced terroir, where the fine retention of silts dialogues with the natural drainage of the stones.

domain.title: Geneva is not a detail. It is the subject.

men.alt: Photo of a young woman drinking a glass of wine with Damien Simone in the background

damien.text:
With no family estate, no viticultural heritage, he built himself alone. A graduate of Changins, Haute École de Viticulture et Œnologie, then trained in the cellars of recognised estates in the canton before deciding it was time to create his own.



He tends the vines, crafts the wines and has sat for several years as a tasting judge at some of the most prestigious competitions in the wine world: the Sélection des Vins de Genève, the Concours Général Agricole de Paris, the Grand Prix des Vins Suisses and the International Wine Challenge in London. His obsession: excellence at every stage, from the plot to the bottle.
damien.title: Damien Simone – Oenologist

men.intro: Domaine Simone was born from a simple conviction: to produce wines that meet the highest standards of excellence.
men.introDamien: Damien Simone, an oenologist trained at Changins, leads every stage of the estate alone, from vine to cellar, with the precision gained over the years and solid experience as a judge at leading wine competitions.
men.introNatalio: By his side, Natalio Cano brings rigour, strategy and direction, ensuring that every wine finds its rightful place at the table. He is the one who structures, who builds relationships with restaurateurs, wine merchants and export markets.

natalio.text:
Rigorous, methodical and deeply committed to the success of the estate, he ensures its structure and direction with a keen sense of precision.



He is the one who builds partnerships with restaurateurs, wine merchants and export markets, and who ensures that every bottle finds the tables it deserves. His dedication and rigour have allowed the estate to establish itself quickly and in the best conditions.
natalio.title: Natalio Cano – Operating Partner

men.outro:
Together, they chose to plant in Russin: an appellation with some of the strictest rules in the canton of Geneva, on slopes that complicate every viticultural gesture and grant no concessions. Here, yields are regulated, practices monitored, and the specifications leave no room for mediocrity.



That is precisely what the two partners came looking for. Not a convenient terroir, but a demanding one. The kind that, when mastered, produces wines of rare precision and depth. Russin is not a default choice. It is a choice of conviction.
men.title: One does not build a reputation to sell wine. One makes wine. The rest follows.

content.title: General Terms and Conditions – Domaine Simone SARL

reserve.intro: This is the founding wine, the one that carries the estate's ambition and sets its standard of excellence. It bears an uncompromising vision: to reach what the terroir, Man and time can offer at their highest, in the spirit of the family crest's motto, Sic itur ad astra. The cornerstone of the project, Calvaire "Grande Réserve" expresses our will to create a wine that is at once a work of art, a strong identity and an international reference.

properties.harvest: Hand-harvested in 13 kg crates, on September 16th, 24th and 25th
properties.maturing: 36 months in new white oak and French oak barrels and 12 months in bottle
properties.soil: Clay-silt with abundant colluvium, from Quaternary glacial deposits (moraines)
properties.variety: 33% Merlot, 33% Cabernet Sauvignon and 34% Cabernet Franc

reserve.quote: We make no compromises. We make wine.

selection.intro: Le Calvaire "Sélection" is the first step of a story we intend to inscribe in time. A wine born from a simple conviction: greatness is built, not claimed. Through it, we seek less to seduce than to reveal a place, a tension, an identity. It is a promise we make, and one that every Collection will have to deserve.

properties.alcoholicFermentation: In stainless steel tank at low temperature (< 20°C)
properties.harvest: Hand-harvested in 13 kg crates, on September 8th and 11th
properties.maturing: 6 months on fine lees with regular stirring, in stainless steel tank, with a fraction (16% of the Chardonnay) aged in new white oak barrels (Quercus Alba).
properties.soil: Clay-silt with abundant colluvium, from Quaternary glacial deposits (moraines)

selection.quote: This wine tells a story: that of an underestimated terroir and a man determined to reveal it.
